description:
You are a compliance officer performing a quick audit of server patch levels.

1. In the following directory, a status file already exists:
   /home/user/systems/patch_status.txt

   • Each line in this file is a comma-separated record with two fields:
     hostname,patch_level  
     – hostname  : a single word, consisting only of letters and numbers  
     – patch_level : either the literal string “current” or “outdated”

   • Example (this is the real, pre-populated content of the file on your system):
     serverA,current  
     serverB,outdated  
     serverC,current  
     serverD,outdated  
     serverE,current  

2. Your task is to prepare a very small compliance artefact named:
   /home/user/compliance/non_compliant_servers.log

   The file must meet ALL of these requirements:

   a. It must contain ONLY the hostnames whose patch_level is “outdated”.
   b. Each hostname must be on its own line with **no** additional columns, spaces, or punctuation.
   c. The hostnames must be sorted in standard ASCII ascending order (i.e. the same order produced by the command ‘sort’ with default settings).
   d. The file must end with a single trailing newline character.

3. This is a “single-command” mini-exercise: you can accomplish the task using one shell pipeline (though using a temporary subshell, redirection, pipes, etc., is fine).

4. When you are done, you should be able to display the file and see exactly:

   serverB  
   serverD  

   …with nothing else before, between, or after those two lines other than a final newline.

The automated tests will:
• Verify that /home/user/compliance/non_compliant_servers.log exists.  
• Confirm its contents match the precise format described above.  
• Ensure there are no extra characters (spaces, commas, blank lines, etc.).

Produce the file accordingly. You do NOT need to output anything in this chat besides the usual terminal echo; the verification will read the file directly from the filesystem.
truth:
Initial state provided to the agent:

1. Directory hierarchy:
   /home/user/
       systems/
           patch_status.txt
       compliance/            ← directory already exists and is writable

2. Exact contents of /home/user/systems/patch_status.txt (5 lines, each ending with newline):
serverA,current
serverB,outdated
serverC,current
serverD,outdated
serverE,current

3. Expected artefact after successful task completion:
   Path: /home/user/compliance/non_compliant_servers.log  
   Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--) – default for a newly created file in the agent’s umask environment.  
   Content (including the final newline, no leading/trailing spaces):

serverB
serverD

4. The automated test will:
   • Check that the file exists at the exact path.  
   • Read its contents and compare byte-for-byte to the two-line string above, ensuring a trailing newline is present.  
   • Fail if there are any other characters, additional lines, or missing newline at end-of-file.
name: task_000000_84a2ba7d
